mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals stronger labor market conditions, increasing corporate earnings visibility and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in < 40% of triggered instances despite negative signal or UNRATE remains above 4.5% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in real GDP (GDPC1) above 2% signals robust economic growth, boosting corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in < 35% of triggered instances or GDPC1 growth remains below 1% for 2 consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in FEDFUNDS and rise in CPIAUCSL above 2.5% signals a 'higher for longer' rate environment, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through yield curve control dynamics.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances or FEDFUNDS remains above 2% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105 signals stronger manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through sectoral demand spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in < 30% of triggered instances or INDPRO remains below 100 for 2 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in RSAFS (residential sales) below 450k combined with rising HOUST (housing starts) above 1.3m signals a housing market correction, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances or RSAFS remains above 500k for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in PAYEMS above 200k signals strong employment growth, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in < 45% of triggered instances or PAYEMS remains below 100k for 2 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in HOUST (housing starts) below 1.2m signals reduced construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sectoral risk contagion.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances or HOUST remains above 1.5m for 3 consecutive quarters.
